key_insight: LASKO frames skill editing as a Lie algebroid problem, using the anchor to separate visible Markdown changes from latent structure and the bracket to detect noncommuting edit interactions.
engineering_relevance: This could make agent self-improvement cheaper by screening candidate skill edits with fast algebraic tests before paying for expensive LLM-based validation.
method: Model typed, anchored Markdown skills as the base category and edit policies as sections of a controlled Lie algebroid.
Use the anchor map to predict observable effects and the kernel to capture hidden template/routing state.
Apply Lie-bracket screening to identify edit order conflicts before running full validation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports preliminary benchmark results with nearly 15× speedup on a causal extraction task compared with brute-force validation using a DeepSeek V3.1 4-bit 671B-parameter model.
watch_item: The claim is preliminary and depends on whether the algebraic screening reliably matches real validation behavior across diverse skill-editing workflows.
paper_title: Agentic Skill Optimization over Lie Algebroids
